mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, compressing valuation multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate increases are not significantly lower than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate crossing above 4.0 percent signals meaningful labor market cooling that alleviates wage inflation concerns, prompting positive short-term equity market reactions.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 55 percent of instances when the unemployment rate exceeds 4.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month expansion in real retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer and investment demand, driving positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in retail sales and housing starts fails to predict positive 60-day returns with accuracy exceeding 50 percent over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle signals supply-side bottlenecks and economic deceleration, leading to negative monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: Industrial production declines during tightening periods are followed by positive 20-day returns in more than 60 percent of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index readings exceeding 285.0 reflect entrenched inflation that forces markets to price in aggressive future policy tightening, causing immediate negative equity returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55 percent of observations when the consumer price index exceeds the 285.0 threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls reflects robust economic activity and expanding corporate earnings potential, generating positive quarterly equity market returns.
failure_condition: Payroll employment growth shows no statistical correlation with 60-day forward returns, with Rank IC remaining below 0.01 over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly real sales indicates inventory accumulation or underlying demand weakness, forecasting negative monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: GDP and real sales divergence fails to predict negative returns, with classification accuracy falling below 45 percent over rolling 3-year evaluation periods.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production expansion during a post-shock recovery phase reflects resolving supply chain constraints and stabilizing input costs, driving positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth during recovery phases yields negative 5-day returns in more than 60 percent of historical instances.
